---
title: 'Answer to: Stress, Distress, and Depression'
date: '2012-01-06T07:21:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '7869054142589969823'
blogger_filename: '/2012/01/answer-to-stress-distress-and-depression.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'study'
  - 'Prayer Time'
---

I was reading and studying today. I came&nbsp;across&nbsp;this passage.<br /><br /><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/1-samuel/30-6.html" target="_blank"><b>1 Samuel 30:6 (New King James Version)</b></a><br />6 Now David was greatly distressed, for the people spoke of stoning him, because the soul of all the people was grieved, every man for his sons and his daughters. But David strengthened himself in the Lord his God. <br /><br /><b><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/passage.aspx?q=1-samuel+30:6&amp;t=kjv" target="_blank">1 Samuel 30:6 - Interlinear Bible</a></b><br /><span class="versenum" style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; font-weight: bold; line-height: 20px; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 3px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">6</span><span style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px;">&nbsp;And&nbsp;</span><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=01732" style="background-color: white; color: #3380cc; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px;">David</a><span style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px;">&nbsp;was&nbsp;</span><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=03966" style="background-color: white; color: #336699; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px; text-decoration: none;">greatly</a><span style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px;">&nbsp;</span><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=03334" style="background-color: white; color: #336699; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px; text-decoration: none;">distressed</a><span style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px;">&nbsp;; for the&nbsp;</span><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=05971" style="background-color: white; color: #336699; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px; text-decoration: none;">people</a><span style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px;">&nbsp;</span><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=0559" style="background-color: white; color: #336699; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px; text-decoration: none;">spake</a><span style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px;">&nbsp;of&nbsp;</span><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=05619" style="background-color: white; color: #336699; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px; text-decoration: none;">stoning</a><span style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px;">&nbsp;him, because the</span><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=05315" style="background-color: white; color: #336699; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px; text-decoration: none;">soul</a><span style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px;">&nbsp;of all the&nbsp;</span><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=05971" style="background-color: white; color: #336699; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px; text-decoration: none;">people</a><span style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px;">&nbsp;was&nbsp;</span><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=04843" style="background-color: white; color: #336699; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px; text-decoration: none;">grieved</a><span style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px;">&nbsp;, every&nbsp;</span><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=0376" style="background-color: white; color: #336699; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px; text-decoration: none;">man</a><span style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px;">&nbsp;for his&nbsp;</span><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=01121" style="background-color: white; color: #336699; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px; text-decoration: none;">sons</a><span style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px;">&nbsp;and for his&nbsp;</span><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=01323" style="background-color: white; color: #336699; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px; text-decoration: none;">daughters:</a><span style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px;">&nbsp;but</span><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=01732" style="background-color: white; color: #336699; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px; text-decoration: none;">David</a><span style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px;">&nbsp;</span><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=02388" style="background-color: white; color: #336699; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px; text-decoration: none;">encouraged</a><span style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px;">&nbsp;himself in the&nbsp;</span><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=03068" style="background-color: white; color: #336699; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px; text-decoration: none;">LORD</a><span style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px;">&nbsp;his&nbsp;</span><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=0430" style="background-color: white; color: #336699; font-family: arial; font-size: 16px; line-height: 20px; text-decoration: none;">God.</a><br /><br /><b>Encourage/Strengthen here is the Hebrew Word:&nbsp;Chazaq /&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/interlinear-bible/strongs.ashx?ll=h&amp;t=kjv&amp;sn=02388" style="background-color: white; color: #336699; font-family: BSTHebrew; font-size: 24px; line-height: 20px; text-align: right; text-decoration: none;">qeZ;x.tiY;w</a></b><br />"to strengthen, prevail, harden, be strong, become strong, be courageous, be firm, grow firm, be resolute, be sore"<br /><br />Sometimes following great victory there arises a situation or circumstance that could open the door for distress or depression. The devil wants nothing more than to steal your joy and victory. I have found in my own life a&nbsp;roller coaster&nbsp;effect. One minute I feel as though I could&nbsp;conquer&nbsp;the world and nothing could stop me. An hour later the wrong glance from my wife or news that something didn't happen the way I planned or expected and I feel that I am never going to get where I need to be. This roller coaster comes from a lack of perspective. How often have I looked back on a time of stress and distress, or even depression, and wondered: "Now why was I so concerned about that?"<br /><br />Like in&nbsp;high school&nbsp;when an&nbsp;embarrassing&nbsp;event&nbsp;happened&nbsp;and I just knew I could never return to school again. If the same thing happened to me today I might either play it off or maybe not even truly care about it at all. Perspective.<br /><br />What did David do here? He encouraged himself in the Lord. He strengthened himself. How did he do this? One thing we can assume from his hundreds of Psalms is that he probably sang, if only to himself. He reminded himself of the goodness of God. He inquired of the Lord.<br /><br />It's not God's responsibility to encourage me if I am depressed or distressed. It's mine. I must encourage MYSELF to live in constant victory.<br /><br />Notice that He was distressed, emotionally. His wife's were missing. He was terrified for their well being. Finally he got a hold of himself and rose up to take authority over the situation and get them back!<br /><br />Verse 8 And David enquired at the LORD, saying , Shall I pursue after this troop? shall I overtake them? <br /><br />David took action. Paul said it this way: <a href="http://1828.mshaffer.com/d/search/word,stir" target="_blank">Stir Yourself Up... to stir is to agitate, mix, and move around</a>, <a href="http://1828.mshaffer.com/d/search/word,stir" target="_blank">Websters 1828 Dictionary</a>.<br /><br /><br /><h4 class="ResultTitle" style="background-color: white; clear: left; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 17px; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/kjv/2-timothy/1-6.html" style="color: #3380cc;">2 Timothy 1:6 KJV</a></h4><div class="ResultText" style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 14px; margin-bottom: 3px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">Wherefore I put thee in remembrance that thou&nbsp;<span class="Highlight" style="background-color: #ffff96; font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 2px; padding-right: 2px; padding-top: 0px;">stir</span>&nbsp;<span class="Highlight" style="background-color: #ffff96; font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 2px; padding-right: 2px; padding-top: 0px;">up</span>&nbsp;the gift of God, which is in thee by the putting on of my hands.</div><br /><br /><br /><h4 class="ResultTitle" style="background-color: white; clear: left; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 17px; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/kjv/2-peter/1-13.html" style="color: #3380cc;">2 Peter 1:13 KJV</a></h4><div class="ResultText" style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 14px; margin-bottom: 3px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">Yea, I think it meet, as long as I am in this tabernacle, to&nbsp;<span class="Highlight" style="background-color: #ffff96; font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 2px; padding-right: 2px; padding-top: 0px;">stir</span>&nbsp;you&nbsp;<span class="Highlight" style="background-color: #ffff96; font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 2px; padding-right: 2px; padding-top: 0px;">up</span>&nbsp;by putting you in remembrance;</div><br /><br /><br /><h4 class="ResultTitle" style="background-color: white; clear: left; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 17px; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/kjv/2-peter/3-1.html" style="color: #3380cc;">2 Peter 3:1 KJV</a></h4><div class="ResultText" style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 14px; margin-bottom: 3px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">This second epistle, beloved, I now write unto you; in both which I&nbsp;<span class="Highlight" style="background-color: #ffff96; font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 2px; padding-right: 2px; padding-top: 0px;">stir</span>&nbsp;<span class="Highlight" style="background-color: #ffff96; font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 2px; padding-right: 2px; padding-top: 0px;">up</span>&nbsp;your pure minds by way of remembrance:</div><br /><br />When stress and distress come on you start to agitate the Word of God in you by calling it to&nbsp;remembrance&nbsp;and repeating it aloud. Mix it with Faith and call those things which be not as though they are. And move around physically. The act of physical&nbsp;movement&nbsp;has a&nbsp;definite&nbsp;impact on the mind and spirit. Start walking around the room preaching to yourself the Word of God. Sing, Dance, Praise God despite your feelings. Watch the atmosphere chance around you as you become the&nbsp;thermostat&nbsp;instead of the thermometer of the situation.<br /><br />Darrell G. Wolfe
